form.targetRoutingDesc: Configure data storage mode per equipment
form.apiUriAuto: API URI is auto-synced from Settings page server port on save.

tooltip.dataDir: Directory where Vector stores internal data such as disk buffers and checkpoints. The folder is auto-created if it doesn't exist.
tooltip.apiIp: Binding IP for Vector's own status/health API. The dashboard uses this to check Vector status. 0.0.0.0 allows access from all networks.
tooltip.apiPort: Port for Vector's status API. Default: 8687 (Agents use 8686). The dashboard 'Infrastructure' panel queries this port to check Vector's status.
tooltip.listenIp: IP address to listen for incoming logs from Agent (equipment) PCs. 0.0.0.0 allows connections from all networks — required since Agents connect externally.
tooltip.listenPort: Port for receiving Agent logs. Must match the address port in Agent configs. E.g., Agent sends to 'serverIP:6000' → this listens on 6000.
tooltip.rawPath: Path pattern for saving raw log files. Supports variables: {{ equipment_type }}, {{ equipment_id }}, {{ filename }}, and date variables %Y (year), %m (month), %d (day).
tooltip.rawBufSize: Max disk buffer size for raw file storage. When full, reception pauses to prevent data loss. Set according to available disk space.
tooltip.apiUri: Endpoint URL of the Node.js API server (Fastify) that receives parsed log data and stores it in Oracle DB. Default: http://127.0.0.1:3100/api/logs
tooltip.batchMax: Max events to batch before sending. Sends immediately when this count is reached. Higher values improve efficiency but may increase latency.
tooltip.batchTimeout: Max seconds to wait before sending a partial batch. Prevents data delay. E.g., set to 5 = guaranteed delivery within 5 seconds.
tooltip.apiBufSize: Max disk buffer size for API transmission. If the Node.js server is down, data is retained in this buffer and auto-resent on recovery.
tooltip.retryInitial: Seconds to wait before the first retry after a failed API transmission. Each subsequent retry waits longer (exponential backoff).
tooltip.retryMax: Maximum retry interval in seconds. Even with exponential backoff, the wait time won't exceed this value. E.g., 30 = retries at most every 30s.
tooltip.targetType: How data is stored in Oracle. TABLE performs direct INSERT, PROCEDURE calls a stored procedure.
tooltip.targetTable: Target table or procedure name. Leave empty to use the LOG_{log_type} default pattern.
tooltip.timestampMode: Timestamp generation method. now() uses receive time, source field uses the time value from the log.
tooltip.logStructure: Log file structure type. Single Row = 1 record per file, Multi Row = header + data rows CSV, Multi Section = multiple structures in one file (e.g., AOI BoardInfo + Inspection).
tooltip.headerLines: Number of header lines in the CSV file. Data rows are parsed starting after this many lines.
tooltip.sectionMarkers: Enter section delimiter keywords separated by commas. Each section can have a different column structure.
tooltip.apiCodec: Encoding format for data sent to API server. json is the default and recommended for most cases.
tooltip.fileCodec: Encoding format for raw file storage. text saves only the original message, json includes metadata.
tooltip.httpMethod: HTTP method for sending data to the API server. POST is used in most cases.
tooltip.compression: Compression method for transmitted data. Use gzip if network bandwidth is limited.
tooltip.apiBufType: Storage location for API transmission buffer. disk is safe but slower, memory is fast but lost on process exit.
tooltip.apiBufPolicy: Behavior when buffer is full. block waits until sent, drop_newest discards new data.
tooltip.fileBufType: Storage location for file write buffer. disk is safe but slower, memory is fast but lost on process exit.
tooltip.fileBufPolicy: Behavior when buffer is full. block waits until written, drop_newest discards new data.
tooltip.healthcheck: Enable API server healthcheck. Automatically pauses transmission if the server is unresponsive.
tooltip.acknowledgements: Enable delivery acknowledgements. Retains data at source until delivery is confirmed.

sender.title: SENDER
sender.subtitle: Per-Equipment Agent TOML Config
sender.deleteMsg: config file will be permanently deleted. Continue?

fluent.pathHint: Separate multiple paths with commas (e.g. C:\logs\*.txt,C:\logs\*.csv)

tooltip.flush: How often to flush (send) logs. Shorter = more real-time but more load. Recommended: 1s
tooltip.logLevel: Fluent Bit's own log level. error: errors only, info: general, debug/trace: verbose
tooltip.storagePath: Internal storage path for Fluent Bit. Buffers, position tracking DB, etc.
tooltip.storageSync: Data file sync mode. normal: standard, full: sync on every write (safer but slower)
tooltip.refreshInterval: How often to scan for new log files (seconds). Recommended: 5s
tooltip.flushTimeout: Multiline parser timeout (ms). Event completes after no new lines for this duration. Recommended: 1000ms
tooltip.tag: Tag attached to events. Used by [FILTER] and [OUTPUT] Match rules.
tooltip.readFromHead: true: read from file beginning, false: read only newly appended content
tooltip.db: SQLite DB path for tracking read position. Required for resume-on-restart.
tooltip.multiline: On: enable multiline parser (merge entire file into one event), Off: line-by-line events

tooltip.equipType: Major equipment category (e.g., AOI, SPI, MOUNTER).
tooltip.logType: Type of logs to collect (e.g., INSPECTION, ALARM, PROCESS).
tooltip.lineCode: Identifier for the line where the equipment is located.
tooltip.equipId: Unique management ID or name of the equipment.
tooltip.equipIp: IP address of the equipment PC. Displayed on the equipment dashboard.
tooltip.serverIp: IP address of the Receiver (Aggregator) server.
tooltip.serverPort: Port number the Receiver server is listening on for logs.
tooltip.readFrom: Initial read position. 'beginning' starts from the start of files; 'end' starts from new logs added after config.
tooltip.ignoreOlder: Ignore log files older than the specified seconds.
tooltip.bufferSize: Max size of logs to keep in memory during transmission delays.
tooltip.logPaths: Paths where log files are generated. Wildcards (*) are supported (e.g., C:\logs\*.csv).
tooltip.wholeFile: ON: Send the entire file (header+data) as a single event (e.g., CSV). OFF: Send each line as a separate event.
tooltip.dataDir: Directory where Vector stores internal data such as disk buffers and checkpoints.
tooltip.apiPort: Port for Vector's own status API. Default: 8686. Used by the dashboard to check Agent status.
tooltip.fpStrategy: File dedup strategy. checksum hashes initial lines; device_and_inode uses filesystem metadata.
tooltip.fpLines: Number of initial lines used for checksum calculation. Higher values are more accurate but slower.
tooltip.mlMode: Multiline merge mode. halt_before: break before pattern, halt_with: break including pattern, continue_through: keep merging while condition matches.
tooltip.mlStart: Multiline start pattern (regex). Lines matching this pattern begin a new event.
tooltip.mlCond: Multiline condition pattern (regex). Used with mode to determine event boundaries.
tooltip.bufType: Buffer storage location. disk is safe but slower, memory is fast but lost on process exit.
tooltip.bufPolicy: Behavior when buffer is full. block waits until sent, drop_newest discards new data.
tooltip.compression: Compression method for data sent to Aggregator. Use gzip if network bandwidth is limited.
tooltip.acknowledgements: Enable delivery acknowledgements. Retains data until the Aggregator confirms receipt.
tooltip.heartbeat: Enable heartbeat to periodically send status via Aggregator. Allows the dashboard to monitor equipment online/offline status in real-time.
tooltip.heartbeatInterval: Heartbeat interval in seconds. Lower values detect status faster but increase network load. Recommended: 30s
tooltip.resendPath: Resend folder path. Files copied here are automatically sent and deleted.
tooltip.resendDeleteSecs: Seconds to wait before deleting a file after successful transmission.
